algorithms.title: Course Capstone: Systematic Approach for Any Medical Patient
algorithms.description: The unifying assessment sequence applicable across every chapter of this course, including oncologic and complex presentations.
steps: Scene size-up: safety, precautions, and check for a shared-cause pattern if multiple patients present similarly.
Primary assessment: general impression, LOC, identify and treat life threats (A-B-C) first, regardless of known diagnoses.
Secondary assessment: history-heavy (about 80% of the answer), using active listening (get on the patient's level, ask follow-up questions).
Build a differential diagnosis from possibilities to probabilities — use known conditions (e.g., cancer, diabetes, dialysis) as context, not as an automatic explanation.
In oncology patients with dyspnea, actively consider pleural effusion and cardiac tamponade alongside disease progression, PE, and infection.
Trust your own exam findings even when the history is complex or the chief complaint is vague.
Reassess continuously and treat/transport per the leading diagnosis identified.
